design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate grid-based puzzle rooms by pushing/collecting items, defeating enemies with strategic placement, and reaching the exit. Each room presents a self-contained spatial logic problem.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Environmental constraints vs. player agency: limited resources, enemy patterns, and irreversible puzzle elements (crumbling bridges) create decision pressure within discrete puzzle spaces.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master logical problem-solving through experimentation and spatial reasoning; progress as a hero (Lolo/Lala) rescuing petrified friends from an antagonist.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Puzzle-first design philosophy with no time pressure or execution demand. The game respects player agency by allowing infinite restarts within levels without penalty, positioning failure as learning iteration rather than punishment. Character choice (Lolo vs Lala) is cosmetic, emphasizing puzzle mechanics over character progression.

difficulty.lives_system: No lives limit. Pressing Select restarts current room without penalty or death state.
difficulty.continues_system: Infinite continues via Select button. No game-over state exists for progression failure.
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-room checkpointing. Pressing Start returns to overworld map; pressing Start again reveals 16-character password for that progression point.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Very high. Players control failure recovery entirely—restart rooms freely, skip to any unlocked level via password system, choose character (though functionally identical). No difficulty modifiers or assist modes documented, but infinite restart mechanism serves as implicit difficulty accessibility.

progression.unlock_mechanism: 17 sequential levels organized by increasing complexity. Levels 4–7, 9–12, and 17 contain boss encounters. Training rooms (10 total) located in two overworld trees serve as optional skill-building chambers. Password system allows non-linear access to any previously reached point.
replayability_hooks: Two character skins (Lolo/Lala) with different dialogue in tutorial rooms
Training rooms separate from main progression for skill refinement
100 total puzzle rooms suggest high puzzle variety within 17 levels

world_building_techniques: Opening premise: Great King's son petrifies friends; framing the antagonist as a court heir raises questions of nobility and conflict
Character dialogue differs between Lolo and Lala, personalizing tutorial and narrative beats
Overworld map structure (two trees, level progression) suggests geographic world with distinct locations

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters punctuate level progression (9 boss rooms across levels 4–7, 9–12, and 17). Likely function as spatial puzzle challenges similar to standard rooms, requiring enemy pattern recognition and strategic item/enemy placement rather than execution-based combat.

danger_signals: Petrification spell (irreversible status effect indicating high-stakes magic)
Moby enemies in underwater levels using line-of-sight suction attacks (environmental hazard hazard escalation)
environmental_storytelling: Overworld map with two distinct trees (training vs progression geography)
Underwater levels (new environment type with distinct enemy types)
Crumbling bridges (irreversible environmental obstacles suggesting time/resource pressure within individual puzzles)
